title: Strategic rules deviations S2 and S3 in post-combat loss counting

changes.S2: cleanUpShips() now increments m_lostSatharShips for every destroyed Sathar ship including fighters (removed the getType() != 'Fighter' guard on that counter).
changes.S3: cleanUpShips() increments m_lostTendaySathar / m_lostTendayUPF only when the destroyed ship is neither a fighter nor a member of a militia fleet (fleet->isMilitia()), applied on both sides.
changes.unchanged: Conditions 1/2 (m_lostAC/m_lostHC), removal/ownership logic, and save()/load() left intact.
changes.doxygen: cleanUpShips() header updated (Last Modified Jul 11, 2026; author list; condition-3/condition-5 counting rules documented).

validation.notes: 10 FGameHeaderDependencyTest failures only appear when SSWTests is run from a cwd other than tests/ (cwd-relative source-inspection paths); pre-existing and unrelated to this change.
